what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 SVG line icon made of two stroked paths that visually suggest a simplified handshake or interlocking hands.
observations: Uses an inline SVG root with xmlns and viewBox="0 0 24 24".
Sets fill="none" and stroke="currentColor" so the icon inherits color from its context.
Uses stroke-width="1.5" with round line caps and joins.
Contains two path elements drawn with straight line commands.
standouts: Angular geometry represents the handshake without filled shapes.
conventions: Uses rounded stroke caps and joins for a softened line-icon style.
Keeps the asset self-contained with no external references.
concerns: No title, desc, role, or aria attributes are present for standalone accessibility context.
notes: A minimal, themeable SVG handshake icon built from two angular stroked paths.
questions: Is this intended to be decorative-only or should consuming code provide an accessible label?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename indicates a simple handshake icon, and the SVG content matches that purpose.
intention_signals: It appears intended as a reusable design-system icon for representing agreement, partnership, or connection in a lightweight visual form.
novelty: The handshake is reduced to two interlocking zigzag strokes rather than detailed hand outlines.
contribution: Contributes a compact currentColor handshake symbol that a fused system could keep as a reusable, style-adaptable icon asset.
summary_for_embedding: This is a minimal 24x24 SVG handshake icon composed of two rounded, currentColor stroked paths with no fill.
intention_for_embedding: This was for providing a lightweight, reusable icon that communicates handshake, agreement, or partnership while inheriting surrounding color styling.
